title: lippia crenata
intro: Lippia crenata, also known as Mexican oregano or Mexican marjoram, is a shrubby perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Lippia crenata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Lippia crenata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional cooking and as a natural insect repellent.
